_note: Status 'mapped' means the component has been mapped to this criterion against internal platform standards only. No WCAG or external compliance claim is made. Run audit-rules.json checks and update validation_status before changing overall_status. See analysis.md for full risk and token finding details.

standards.name: Platform Design System — Chip Component Standards
standards._note: Internal platform rules for the Chip component. IDs are stable. See standards/platform-code.standards.json for full definitions.

criteria.title: Component must use design tokens for all visual values
criteria.evidence: chip.css references var(--chip-*) component-scoped tokens exclusively. However, no chip-scoped tokens exist in token.css at this time. All token references are pending resolution. The CSS architecture is correct; the token definitions are missing. See analysis.md §Token Findings.

criteria.title: RTL layout support is mandatory
criteria.evidence: chip.css uses padding-inline (logical property). Physical directional properties (padding-left, padding-right) are absent. RTL direction is inherited from [dir='rtl'] at the container level, consistent with the manifest analysis which confirmed RTL=True produces zero CSS differences in Figma.

criteria.title: Semantic HTML element required
criteria.evidence: <button> is the required root element for interactive chips. <span> is permitted for display-only chips. chip.contract.md §HTML Contract documents all valid element/role combinations. CHIP-STRUCT-001 enforces this at the DOM level.

criteria.title: Minimum touch target 24×24px
criteria.evidence: Small chip height is 20px (var(--chip-sm-height)), below the 24px minimum. Medium (24px) and Large (32px) pass. CHIP-A11Y-004 flags Small size chips. Design team must either raise the minimum height or document an explicit exception.

standards.name: Platform Accessibility Internal Standards
standards._note: Accessibility-specific internal standards. See standards/accessibility.standards.json for full definitions.

criteria.title: Arabic language font support required
criteria.evidence: IBM Plex Sans Arabic is applied via var(--chip-font-family), which maps to var(--font-sans). All label text inherits this token. No override to a Latin-only font is present in chip.css.

criteria.title: Toggle chip must declare aria-pressed or aria-selected
criteria.evidence: chip.css targets [aria-pressed='true'] and [aria-selected='true'] for the Selected visual state. chip.contract.md §Aria Contract documents the rule: Selected = persistent toggle via aria-pressed (standalone) or aria-selected (group context). CHIP-A11Y-003 enforces the presence of at least one attribute when .is-selected is used.

criteria.title: Focus ring must not be suppressed
criteria.evidence: chip.css defines a focus outline via the ::after pseudo-element on :focus and :focus-visible. outline:none is set on the root to suppress browser default, but the custom ::after ring replaces it. CHIP-A11Y-002 catches suppression without replacement. Implementation deviation: inset:-4px used instead of Figma fixed pixel widths to support dynamic content — documented in chip.contract.md §Focus Behavior.

criteria.title: No hardcoded color, spacing, or radius values in component CSS
criteria.evidence: chip.css contains no hardcoded hex, rgb(), or hsl() values. All visual properties reference var(--chip-*) semantic tokens. However, those tokens are not yet defined in token.css — they are pending. The CSS structure is compliant; the token definitions are the blocking gap. CHIP-TOKEN-001 will pass once token.css is updated.

variant_compliance_notes.chip--sm: PLATFORM-CODE-CHIP-004: Small chip height is 20px, below the 24px internal minimum touch target. Requires design team sign-off or a raised height token before this variant can be marked compliant.
variant_compliance_notes.chip--on-color: Contrast cannot be verified without knowing the deployment background color. On-color chips must be verified per-deployment to confirm adequate contrast between chip surface and background.
variant_compliance_notes.disabled (all variants): Low contrast of the disabled state is intentional. Inactive UI components are exempt from active-state contrast requirements per internal accessibility standards.
variant_compliance_notes.chip--primary pressed: Text color for Primary/Pressed (--chip-text-primary-pressed) previously referenced a Button-namespaced token in Figma. A chip-scoped token is required. See analysis.md §KI-6.
